steps.title: Apply the fractional exponent rule
steps.content: For any nonnegative base $u$, $$u^{1/2} = \sqrt{u}.$$ With $u = 133y$, this gives $$(133y)^{1/2} = \sqrt{133y}.$$

steps.title: Evaluate each choice
steps.content:
• A: $133 \cdot \sqrt{y}$ equals $\sqrt{133^2 \cdot y} = \sqrt{17689y}$, not $\sqrt{133y}$. Eliminate.
  • B: $\sqrt{133} \cdot y$ equals $\sqrt{133 \cdot y^2} = \sqrt{133y^2}$, not $\sqrt{133y}$. Eliminate.
  • C: $\sqrt{133y}$ matches the derived expression. Keep.
  • D: $\sqrt{(133y)^2} = 133y$ (for $y > 0$), which is not equivalent to $\sqrt{133y}$. Eliminate.
That matches choice C — C is correct.
